user_query:
I run a local framing shop and I’m sponsoring ballet patrons this season. Please set up an event-triggered automation that watches the input/incoming/ directory for new .html flyers and updates my calendar and alerts. Use the provided sample files to bootstrap and produce outputs now.

Requirements:
- Create a command-line script under scripts/ (any language is fine) that can:
  1) Run once to process existing files, and
  2) Watch input/incoming/ for newly added .html files and process them as they appear.
- On each run (or when a new file arrives), inspect these directories:
  - input/incoming/ (new flyers)
  - input/archive/ (older flyers)
- Parse each HTML file’s event listings. Each listing is a div.event-card with:
  - data-event-id attribute (unique event id)
  - h2.title (event title)
  - span.category (event category)
  - time[datetime] (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD)
  - span.venue (venue name)
- Write/update output/schedule.csv with header: event_id,title,category,date,venue,source_file. Append only events whose event_id is not already present; keep existing rows.
- Use input/config.yaml for settings (today, event_window_days, discount_code, keywords). Treat “today” as the date in the config, not the system date.
- Produce output/alerts.json as an array of objects for upcoming ballet events occurring within event_window_days from today. Consider an event “ballet” if category equals "Ballet" (case-insensitive) or the title contains any keyword from config (case-insensitive). Each alert object must include: event_id,title,date,venue,discount_code,source_file.
- Append a simple processing summary to output/logs/run.log (e.g., files processed and counts of new/duplicate events).
- Do not use the Internet; work only with the local files provided and future files dropped into input/incoming/.
- After implementing, run the script once in one-off mode against the provided sample inputs so the outputs exist under output/.

Inputs:
- input/config.yaml (settings)
- input/incoming/ballet_spring_gala.html (new flyer)
- input/archive/arts_february.html (older flyer)
- input/schedule.csv (existing calendar entries)

Outputs:
- output/schedule.csv (updated calendar with new non-duplicate events from both directories)
- output/alerts.json (upcoming ballet alerts with discount)
- output/logs/run.log (run summary)

Key checks:
- Deduplication by event_id against the existing output/schedule.csv content.
- alerts.json includes only ballet events within the configured window from today.
- The watch mode must handle future files added to input/incoming/ in the same HTML structure. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
